mission.title: Install and Configure Kpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: kpt is a package-centric toolchain that automates Kubernetes configuration editing, simplifying the management of Kubernetes platforms and KRM-driven infrastructure. Installing kpt allows you to leverage a powerful CLI for managing configuration as data, enhancing your Kubernetes experience.

steps.title: Add the kpt Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official kpt Helm repository to your local Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add kpt https://charts.kpt.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install kpt using Helm
steps.description:
Install kpt in a new namespace called 'kpt-system':
```bash
helm install kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt-system --create-namespace --version v1.0.0-beta.61
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the kpt pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kpt-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the kpt deployment:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment kpt --namespace kpt-system --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access the kpt CLI
steps.description:
You can now access the kpt CLI by port-forwarding the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kpt --namespace kpt-system 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the kpt pod running in the 'kpt-system' namespace, and you will be able to access the kpt CLI through port-forwarding. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly on the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kpt https://charts.kpt.dev
helm repo update
helm install kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt-system --create-namespace --version v1.0.0-beta.61
kubectl set resources deployment kpt --namespace kpt-system --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the kpt release from the kpt-system namespace using Helm to remove the deployment and associated resources:
```bash
helm uninstall kpt --namespace kpt-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation:
```bash
kubectl delete crd --all
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace kpt-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the kpt-system namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kpt-system
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources in the kpt-system namespace:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kpt-system -o yaml > kpt-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the kpt release to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt-system --version v1.0.0-beta.62
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the kpt pod is running with the upgraded version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kpt-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the kpt pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kpt-system
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during upgrade, ensure that the version specified is available in the repository:
```bash
helm search repo kpt/kpt --versions
``` 
If the desired version is not listed, update the repo or choose a different version.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable after port-forwarding
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the kpt CLI after port-forwarding, check if the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kpt-system
``` 
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and that the port-forward command is still active.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing pod eviction
troubleshooting.description:
If the kpt pod is being evicted due to resource limits, check the events for the pod:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kpt-system
``` 
Adjust the resource limits if necessary to ensure the pod has enough resources to run.

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
